sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: GE HealthCare stock closes at $84 after new FDA MRI clearances — what investors watch next week
feed.summary: GE HealthCare (NYSE: GEHC) closed at $84.23, up 1.47%, after receiving FDA 510(k) clearances for two new MRI systems and an AI-powered workflow platform. Investors will be monitoring upcoming management presentations on February 26 and March 10 for insights into orders and margins, especially concerning hospital spending on imaging systems and the integration of the Intelerad acquisition. The company is also seeking CE Mark approval in Europe for these new systems by the end of 2026.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) Price Target Raised to $45.00 at Roth Mkm
feed.summary: Roth Mkm has increased its price target for Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) to $45.00 from $39.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, which suggests a potential 13.24% downside from the current share price. The company recently reported Q4 EPS that beat estimates but missed revenue, and management highlighted significant debt reduction. Despite mixed analyst views, the stock currently holds an average "Hold" rating with a consensus target of $49.05.

feed.title: Stellantis Stock: EV Reset, Big Buybacks and a Dividend US Investors Can’t Ignore
feed.summary: Stellantis N.V. is presented as a polarizing global auto stock, offering deep value and high cash returns amid slowing EV demand and aggressive cost-cutting. The article explores whether the company is a strong value play for US investors, emphasizing its strong North American performance, generous capital return policy, and disciplined EV strategy. It also addresses the structural headwinds of EV uncertainty, labor costs, and exposure to China and Europe.

feed.title: SolarEdge Technologies (NASDAQ:SEDG) Given New $33.00 Price Target at Deutsche Bank Aktiengesellschaft
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank Aktiengesellschaft has lowered its price target for SolarEdge Technologies (NASDAQ:SEDG) to $33.00 from $35.00, maintaining a "hold" rating, which suggests a potential downside of 12.93%. Despite SolarEdge beating Q4 earnings and revenue estimates, the company remains unprofitable, though some analysts have raised their price targets, indicating mixed sentiment. The stock traded up to $37.90 on Friday, with institutional investors owning a significant portion of its shares.

feed.title: Medtronic: The Investment Appeal of a Medical Device Giant
feed.summary: Medtronic, a leading medical device company, demonstrates strong investment appeal through its diversified business, innovation-driven growth, and stable dividend record. Despite the upcoming diabetes business spinoff, Medtronic projects 5.5% revenue growth for fiscal 2026, driven by new product launches and technological advancements like the Hugo surgical robot. Analysts have mixed views, with some upgrading to Outperform and others maintaining Neutral ratings, but the company's strong financial outlook and institutional investment suggest continued market confidence.

feed.title: EQT (NYSE:EQT) Stock Price Expected to Rise, Wells Fargo & Company Analyst Says
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has raised its price target for EQT (NYSE:EQT) to $70 from $66, maintaining an "overweight" rating, indicating a potential upside of 15.7%. This comes after EQT exceeded Q4 EPS estimates, reported strong free cash flow, and increased its 2026 production guidance, along with boosting its stake in the Mountain Valley Pipeline. Other analysts have also issued positive ratings, contributing to a "Moderate Buy" consensus and a price target of $65.27 for the oil and gas producer.

feed.title: Outraged cruise passengers blast company’s ‘idiotic’ new dress code crackdown
authors: Latest & Breaking News on Fox News
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line is facing backlash from passengers over its new dining dress code policy, which restricts casual wear like shorts and flip-flops in some premium restaurants. While many travelers express frustration, arguing it contradicts the cruise line's "freestyle" and relaxed vacation vibe, others support the stricter rules for bringing "class back to sailing" in higher-end dining venues. The company has acknowledged customer feedback but maintains its stance on the new policy for certain establishments.

feed.title: Citi Trims Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) Target Amid Multifamily Sector Uncertainty
feed.summary: Citi has cut its price target for Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) to $148 from $155, maintaining a Neutral rating, citing concerns about the multifamily sector. Despite this, MAA's CEO Brad Hill noted early signs of improvement in Q4 2025, with increased occupancy and improved lease performance, and projected stronger revenue trends for 2026. The company is also investing in technology, property enhancements, and expanding its development pipeline in Arizona and Virginia.

feed.title: Sprott (NYSE:SII) Issues Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $0.58 EPS
feed.summary: Sprott (NYSE:SII) reported Q4 EPS of $1.11, significantly beating consensus estimates of $0.53 by $0.58. The company also saw its Assets Under Management (AUM) surge to $70.1 billion by February 2026, driven by strong inflows into precious metal trusts and ETFs. Despite missing revenue expectations, the stock price jumped approximately 10% following the earnings release.

feed.title: Cummins Stock Just Popped Off: Is This Old-School Engine Giant Your Next Power Play?
feed.summary: Cummins Inc. (NYSE: CMI) is experiencing renewed interest due to its involvement in three major US trends: AI data centers, clean trucks, and infrastructure spending. The company is actively transitioning from diesel engines to a broader portfolio of natural gas, hydrogen, batteries, and fuel cells, making it a "picks and shovels" play for the energy and AI revolution. Despite being an industrial stock, its strong US footprint, resilient earnings, and dividend payouts are attracting both traditional and younger investors.

feed.title: Marsh & McLennan Stock: Why Wall Street Just Flipped the Script
feed.summary: Marsh & McLennan (MMC) is gaining significant attention from Wall Street analysts, positioning itself as a "boring" but strong long-term investment, especially in US risk, cyber, climate, and insurance pricing. The company, a professional services powerhouse, monetizes global chaos related to natural disasters, cyberattacks, and healthcare costs through its insurance broking, reinsurance, HR, and consulting segments. Despite not being a "get rich overnight" stock, MMC is seen as a defensive, high-quality name providing stable earnings and growing fees, often appearing on lists of "quality compounders" for long-term investors.

feed.title: Jefferies Raises its Price Target on The Williams Companies, Inc. (WMB) to $81 and Maintains a Buy Rating
feed.summary: Jefferies raised its price target on The Williams Companies, Inc. (WMB) to $81 and maintained a Buy rating, citing a "strong" analyst day update and expecting a 12-13% EBITDA CAGR through FY30. UBS also increased its price target to $89, highlighting Williams' significant power generation backlog and its strong position to benefit from rising natural gas demand from power generation and data centers. The company reported Q4 adjusted EPS of 55c and record Adjusted EBITDA of $7.75 billion in 2025, with 2026 Adjusted EBITDA guided at $8.2 billion at the midpoint.

feed.title: Synopsys downgraded to Hold at HSBC on lack of full-year 2026 catalysts
feed.summary: HSBC Global Investment Research has downgraded Synopsys Inc. (NASDAQ: SNPS) to "Hold" from "Buy," citing limited near-term catalysts and mounting headwinds for 2026. The bank lowered its price target and expects 2026 to be a transition period, with growth primarily driven by the Ansys acquisition while core businesses face challenges like geopolitical uncertainty and resource reallocation towards AI. Synopsys's Design IP segment is expected to remain muted, and visibility beyond next year is limited.

feed.title: M&G PLC Invests $1.88 Million in Southern Company
feed.summary: M&G PLC, a major institutional investor, acquired 19,829 shares of Southern Company (NYSE:SO) in the third quarter of 2025, valued at approximately $1.88 million. This investment signals confidence in Southern Company's long-term prospects as a leading electric and gas utility and highlights ongoing institutional interest in the utility sector, particularly given its focus on renewable energy and grid modernization. The move underscores the continued appeal of regulated utility stocks known for their resilience and steady returns.

feed.title: Oppenheimer Raises its Price Target on SharkNinja, Inc. (SN) to $145 from $140 and Maintains an Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has increased its price target for SharkNinja, Inc. (SN) to $145 from $140, maintaining an Outperform rating due to positive outlooks on the company's near- and long-term prospects. This follows SharkNinja's strong Q4 earnings, which showed significant net sales growth and improved adjusted gross margin, particularly in Beauty and Home Environment Appliances. Other analysts like Morgan Stanley and Guggenheim also raised their price targets on SN.

feed.title: TD Cowen raises Digital Realty Trust (DLR) target on strong Q4 performance
feed.summary: TD Cowen has increased its price target for Digital Realty Trust (DLR) following a strong performance in the fourth quarter. The data center REIT demonstrated solid operational results, leading the analyst firm to adjust its outlook positively. This revision reflects confidence in DLR's financial health and future growth prospects.

feed.title: RBC Raises Prologis (PLD) Target as Industrial Real Estate Outlook Improves
feed.summary: RBC Capital analyst Michael Carroll raised his price target for Prologis (PLD) to $135 from $132, reiterating a "Sector Perform" rating after the company's Q4 FFO beat expectations. The upgrade reflects an improved outlook for industrial real estate and Prologis's growing confidence in expanding its private capital business, notably with potential new co-investment funds for data centers. Prologis plans significant development in 2026, with data centers comprising 40% of new projects, a substantial increase from previous years.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser Co.: Is This ‘Boring’ Timber Stock a Silent Cash Machine?
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser Co. (NYSE: WY) is presented as a "boring" but potentially reliable investment for those interested in dividends and real-world assets tied to the US housing market. The article highlights Weyerhaeuser's role as a timber REIT, its sensitivity to housing cycles and interest rates, and its increasing relevance to ESG and climate-conscious investing due to its sustainable forestry practices. It suggests that while not a high-growth stock, Weyerhaeuser offers a stable cash flow and tangible asset base, making it a valuable addition to a diversified portfolio.

feed.title: Takeda Stock: Quiet Pharma Giant That US Investors Keep Missing
feed.summary: Takeda Pharmaceutical Co Ltd is reshaping its pipeline and balance sheet, focusing on high-value therapies despite being underfollowed by US investors. The article details how Takeda's strategic shifts, dividend appeal, and focus on rare diseases and immunology could impact US portfolios. It advises investors to consider the stock's defensive income potential, pipeline optionality, and exposure to FX and policy risks compared to US pharma giants.

feed.title: SanDisk Stock (SNDK) Opinions on Record Year-to-Date Performance
feed.summary: SanDisk (SNDK) has shown record year-to-date performance, rocketing 174% in 2026 and becoming a top performer in computer data storage. Social media users and traders highlight relentless upward momentum and options optimism, with significant insider and congressional trading activity, and strong institutional investor interest. Analysts have issued numerous buy ratings and price targets, indicating a positive outlook for the stock.

feed.title: BMO Reaffirms Bullish View on Equinix (EQIX) amid Strong Operational Performance
feed.summary: BMO Capital has reaffirmed its "Outperform" rating on Equinix (EQIX) and raised its price target to $1,050 from $925, citing stronger-than-expected 2026 guidance and robust demand, particularly driven by artificial intelligence. Equinix's forecast for full-year revenue exceeding market estimates, along with strong bookings growth and expansion into new markets, underscores its positive outlook despite a slight miss in Q4 revenue due to a deferred leasing transaction. The company's role as a digital infrastructure provider benefiting from increased demand for specialized data centers is highlighted.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Industrial Goods Companies: Cardinal Infrastructure Group, Inc. (CDNL) and Quanta Services (PWR)
feed.summary: Analysts have provided insights into two industrial goods companies: Cardinal Infrastructure Group, Inc. (CDNL) and Quanta Services (PWR). D.A. Davidson analyst Brent Thielman maintained a Buy rating on Cardinal Infrastructure Group, Inc. with a $35.00 price target, while Northland Securities' Gus Richard maintained a Hold rating on Quanta Services with a $525.00 price target. The consensus for CDNL is a Strong Buy with a 22.4% upside, whereas PWR has a Moderate Buy consensus with a 2.1% upside.

feed.title: TD Cowen Raises Digital Realty Trust (DLR) Target on Strong Q4 Performance
feed.summary: TD Cowen has raised its price target for Digital Realty Trust (DLR) to $185 from $179, maintaining a Hold rating, following strong fourth-quarter results. The firm anticipates further upside, particularly from higher renewal spreads in 2026. CEO Andy Power highlighted record results in 2025, exceeding guidance on revenue, EBITDA, and core FFO per share, driven by robust leasing activity and significant growth in interconnection and colocation services.
